title: Capnorchis torulosa
taxonomy: Capnorchis torulosa is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms. Capnorchis torulosa is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae. At the genus level, it is a part of the monotypic genus Capnorchis.

intro: Capnorchis torulosa, also known as the "Dwarf Orchid", is a type of orchid species that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Capnorchis torulosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Capnorchis torulosa is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
